codeReview.summary: The implementation correctly addresses both stated objectives: (1) run_benchmarks.py now collects median-of-3 timing samples per scenario with timing-only subprocess runs separated from parity capture, and (2) detect_environment_mismatch() now rejects python_version drift. The changes are well-structured, follow existing code patterns, and include comprehensive test coverage. All validators (ruff, mypy, pytest full suite) passed. Baselines already include python_version from the prerequisite refresh-python-benchmark-baseline feature.

issues.description: run_cmd_timing() duplicates the PYTHONPATH env setup logic from run_cmd_capture(). Could be refactored into a shared helper to reduce maintenance surface, but both functions work correctly as-is.

scenario_timing_warmup_runs() and scenario_timing_should_capture_stdout() use string matching on scenario names ('Max Count Limit'). If scenario names are renamed, the specialized warmup/capture behavior silently falls back to defaults. This is consistent with other string-matching patterns in the file (e.g. 'Count Matches') so it is not a regression in style.

nonBlockingIssues.description: run_cmd_timing() duplicates PYTHONPATH env setup from run_cmd_capture(). Could be refactored into a shared helper.
Scenario-specific warmup/capture functions use string matching on scenario names, which could silently degrade if names change.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The benchmark harness now has two separate subprocess invocation patterns: run_cmd_capture (with stdout text capture) and run_cmd_timing (timing-only, DEVNULL or PIPE). Both independently set up PYTHONPATH. This design decision is not documented in AGENTS.md but is a reasonable benchmark architecture choice.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: benchmarks/run_benchmarks.py lines 138-175: run_cmd_capture() and run_cmd_timing() each construct env with PYTHONPATH independently.

summary: PASS. Both feature objectives are fully implemented: (1) run_benchmarks.py records median-of-3 timing samples per scenario with timing separated from parity capture, emitting rg_samples_s/tg_samples_s arrays and timing_samples_per_scenario in the JSON artifact; (2) detect_environment_mismatch() now checks python_version alongside platform and machine. Tests are comprehensive, all validators passed (491 tests, 14 skipped), and baselines already include python_version from the prerequisite feature. Two non-blocking code style observations noted (duplicated env setup, string-based scenario matching).
